project_setup.what_it_does: Creates a package.json with defaults. The -y flag skips the interactive questionnaire.
project_setup.when_you_use_it: Once, at the start of a new project.

project_setup.what_it_does: Downloads a package into node_modules/ and adds it to dependencies in package.json.
project_setup.when_you_use_it: Whenever you need a new library. Can install multiple: npm i express dotenv node-fetch

project_setup.what_it_does: Installs a package as a devDependency — tools you need while building but not in production.
project_setup.when_you_use_it: For development-only tools like nodemon, linters, test runners.

project_setup.what_it_does: Removes a package from node_modules and package.json.
project_setup.when_you_use_it: When you no longer need a dependency.

running_your_server.what_it_does: Runs the file once. You have to manually restart after every code change.
running_your_server.when_you_use_it: Quick one-off test, or in production.

running_your_server.what_it_does: Runs the file AND watches for changes. Auto-restarts on save.
running_your_server.detail: npx means 'run this package' — it finds nodemon in your local node_modules without needing a global install.
running_your_server.when_you_use_it: During development. This is your daily driver.

running_your_server.what_it_does: Runs whatever script is defined under 'dev' in your package.json scripts block.
running_your_server.detail: You set this up as: "dev": "nodemon server.js" in package.json. It's just a shortcut.
running_your_server.when_you_use_it: Same as above — just shorter to type.

running_your_server.what_it_does: Kills the running process in your terminal.
running_your_server.when_you_use_it: To stop the server before restarting, or when you're done working.

navigating_the_filesystem.what_it_does: Change directory. Moves you into a folder.
navigating_the_filesystem.when_you_use_it: You need to be inside your project folder before running node or npm commands.

navigating_the_filesystem.what_it_does: Print working directory. Shows where you currently are.

navigating_the_filesystem.alias: ls -la (show hidden files too, like .env and .gitignore)
navigating_the_filesystem.when_you_use_it: To see what's in a folder.
Quick way to create config files.

inspecting_and_debugging.what_it_does: Prints the entire contents of a file to the terminal.
inspecting_and_debugging.when_you_use_it: Quick peek at a file without opening an editor.

inspecting_and_debugging.what_it_does: Makes an HTTP request from the terminal and prints the response.
inspecting_and_debugging.when_you_use_it: Testing your API routes without opening a browser.

inspecting_and_debugging.what_it_does: Makes a POST request with a JSON body.
Runs a one-liner of JavaScript. Useful for quick checks.

git_preview.what_it_does: Creates a new git repository in the current folder.
git_preview.when_you_use_it: Once per project, at the start.

git_preview.what_it_does: Stages all changed files for the next commit.
Saves a snapshot of your staged files with a message.
Shows what's changed, what's staged, what's not tracked.

environment.when_you_use_it: Sanity check that Node is installed.

environment.what_it_does: Shows the full path to the node binary.
environment.when_you_use_it: Debugging path issues — 'is my terminal finding the right node?'

environment.what_it_does: Shows all directories your terminal searches for commands.
environment.when_you_use_it: When a command 'isn't found' even though you installed it.

javascript_concepts.what_it_does: The || operator returns the right side if the left side is falsy (undefined, null, 0, NaN, empty string). So if req.query.page is missing or can't be parsed, page defaults to 1.
javascript_concepts.gotcha: || treats 0 and '' as falsy. If 0 is a valid value, use ?? (nullish coalescing) instead: const count = input ?? 0

javascript_concepts.what_it_does: A boolean flag that prevents a function from running while an async operation is still in progress. Set to true before the operation starts, set to false when it finishes.
javascript_concepts.also_known_as: mutex flag, loading lock, request guard, debounce flag
javascript_concepts.why_it_matters: Without it, rapid user actions (double-clicks, fast scrolling) can trigger multiple simultaneous requests, causing duplicate data, race conditions, or wasted API calls.

javascript_concepts.what_it_does: Flips a boolean. !true becomes false, !false becomes true.
common_patterns: if (!hasMore) — 'if there are NOT more pages'

javascript_concepts.what_it_does: A boolean variable that signals a condition or state. Used to control whether loops continue, buttons appear, or functions execute.
javascript_concepts.also_known_as: flag, sentinel, boolean flag, termination condition (when it controls stopping)
examples_in_your_code: hasMore — controls whether Load More button appears
mainBudget.value > 0 — controls whether high-weight phrases can still be picked

javascript_concepts.what_it_does: textContent sets raw text — any HTML tags appear as visible characters. innerHTML parses the string as HTML — tags become actual DOM elements.
javascript_concepts.why_it_matters: This is why your .filter-count em CSS wasn't working. You were setting textContent, so the browser rendered the literal text '20' instead of creating an em element.
